29 cubic feet. 29 cubic feet.
1960-1983. Correspondence, printed materials, publications Departmental files include material concerning course offerings and conferences; fellowships, grants and funding sources; department and director's correspondence; Collection material in English 2075361
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

Katie Cruz
Date completed:

EAD encoding:
Katie Cruz, April 2014
Date modified:
RMC Staff, June 2014
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Departmental files include material concerning course offerings and conferences; fellowships, grants and funding sources; department and director's correspondence; and records of the European Studies Program. Also, correspondence files; files of Prof. Richard N. Rosecrance; and accounts files.
Records of of the Air War in Indochina Study Group, conducted by Raphael Littauer and Norman Uphoff, which led to the publication of The Air War in Indochina, with an introduction by Neil Sheehan, 1972
RELATED MATERIALS
See also the Center for International Studies Research Material, #53-5-1606 and the Ford Foundation Proposal, #53-5-1533.

Cite As:
Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies Records, #53-5-2158. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
In 1961 professor Mario Einaudi led an effort to establish the Cornell Center for International Studies, whose goal was to support and coordinate the university's efforts in all facets of international research and learning. In 1990 the center changed its name to the Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies.
Related Materials
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/555

Scope and Contents
